title: Alternating knots with unknotting number one
why_relevant: Uses the exact irreducibility definition from the problem for positive-definite integral lattices. Its graph-lattice and changemaker-lattice lemmas give concrete techniques for proving that specified vertex-like lattice elements are irreducible.

title: Lattices, graphs, and Conway mutation
why_relevant: Develops the graph-lattice theory on which later irreducibility arguments are based. The paper contains structural results about positive-definite graph lattices, obtuse bases, and how lattice data encodes graph connectivity.

title: On closed 3-braids with unknotting number one
why_relevant: Introduces the half-integer changemaker/embedding style of argument later used with the same notion of irreducible lattice element. Useful for understanding how positive-definite Goeritz or plumbing lattices are constrained by embeddings in standard lattices.

title: The lens space realization problem
why_relevant: Introduces changemaker lattices and the combinatorial analysis of standard bases in positive-definite lattices. These methods are central to later proofs detecting irreducible elements in plumbing and graph lattices.

title: Deficiency symmetries of surgeries in S^3
why_relevant: Generalizes Greene's changemaker theorems to rational surgeries. It gives lattice-embedding and changemaker machinery that is often used to convert definite plumbing problems into finite combinatorial lattice problems.

title: Non-integer surgery and branched double covers of alternating knots
why_relevant: Applies generalized changemaker lattices to alternating-link Goeritz lattices. Its structural arguments about lattices that are simultaneously graph-like and changemaker-like are relevant to proving irreducibility statements for vertices.

title: Bounds on alternating surgery slopes
why_relevant: Studies uniqueness and structure of changemaker lattices arising from sharp definite 4-manifolds. The norm and coefficient restrictions are useful tools for ruling out decompositions of lattice elements.

title: Alternating Surgeries
why_relevant: Contains a consolidated treatment of graph lattices, p/q-changemaker lattices, and the exact irreducibility definition used in the problem. It is especially useful as a reference for the lattice-theoretic lemmas spread across Greene, Gibbons, and McCoy.

title: On the Floer homology of plumbed three-manifolds
why_relevant: Defines negative-definite weighted plumbing graphs and bad vertices; after sign reversal, the condition in the problem is the same bad-vertex condition. The paper's combinatorial handling of definite weighted trees with at most one bad vertex is directly relevant.

title: On the Ozsvath-Szabo invariant of negative definite plumbed 3-manifolds
why_relevant: Develops graded-root and lattice methods for negative-definite plumbed trees, including the bad-vertex framework. The Laufer-sequence style arguments are useful for extracting combinatorial consequences from definiteness.

title: Lattice cohomology of normal surface singularities
why_relevant: Constructs lattice cohomology directly from negative-definite plumbing graphs. Its treatment of the plumbing lattice, weight functions, and graph reductions supplies techniques for reasoning about weighted-tree intersection forms.

title: Heegaard Floer homology and alternating knots
why_relevant: Uses Goeritz matrices of alternating diagrams as definite graph lattices and relates them to sharp four-manifolds. It is a standard source for the definite-lattice viewpoint behind many later graph-lattice irreducibility arguments.

title: Knots with unknotting number one and Heegaard Floer homology
why_relevant: Develops the original Heegaard-Floer obstruction using definite Goeritz forms and lattice correction terms. It is useful background for the Donaldson-plus-lattice embedding techniques used in changemaker proofs.

title: The prism manifold realization problem
why_relevant: Analyzes D-type plumbing lattices and explicitly proves irreducibility properties of their vertex bases. The classification of irreducible vectors in these tree-like lattices gives model arguments for the present problem.

title: The prism manifold realization problem II
why_relevant: Continues the changemaker-lattice analysis for C-type plumbing lattices and uses irreducible elements to compare standard and vertex bases. Its methods are relevant for proving that a weighted-tree basis must contain irreducible elements.

title: The prism manifold realization problem III
why_relevant: Completes the prism-manifold lattice classification and repeatedly uses the same reducible/irreducible criterion for positive-definite lattices. It provides further examples of how plumbing-graph combinatorics force irreducible vertex-basis elements.

title: The lattice of integer flows of a regular matroid
why_relevant: Gives structural results for graphic and regular-matroid flow lattices, a major source of graph-lattice techniques used by Greene. Its circuit/flow viewpoint can help translate decomposability of lattice vectors into connectivity properties of graphs.

title: The irreducible vectors of a lattice: Some theory and applications
why_relevant: Studies a closely related general notion of irreducible lattice vector and connects it to Voronoi-relevant vectors and obtuse superbases. Although the definition is not identical, its lattice-geometric tools may support an independent proof strategy.
